# Tamkibidi Web — Architecture & Structure

## Quick Reference

| Layer                 | Location                  | Purpose                                             | Examples                                                      |
| --------------------- | ------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Pages & Routes**    | `app/`                    | Next.js 16 App Router (directory-based)             | `app/dashboard/page.tsx`, `app/(auth)/login/page.tsx`         |
| **Features**          | `src/features/`           | Domain-driven modules with components, hooks, utils | `src/features/dashboard/`, `src/features/auth/`               |
| **Shared Components** | `src/shared/components/`  | Reusable UI primitives and guards                   | `ui/button.tsx`, `guard/auth-guard.tsx`                       |
| **API Layer**         | `src/shared/api/`         | GraphQL queries, mutations, generated schemas       | `changeOrderStatus.graphql`, `*.schemas.tsx` (auto-generated) |
| **Utilities**         | `src/lib/`, `src/shared/` | Auth context, tokens, formatting helpers            | `auth-context.tsx`, `tokens.ts`, `format.ts`                  |

## Project Structure Priority

### 1. Feature-Based Organization (`src/features/`)

Each feature is self-contained with its own components, hooks, and utilities:

```
src/features/dashboard/
├── components/          # Dashboard-specific UI
│   ├── order-list-card.tsx
│   ├── place-order-button.tsx
│   └── stats-section.tsx
├── hooks/               # Custom hooks for this feature
│   └── use-order-pagination.ts
├── utils/               # Feature-specific helpers
│   └── format.ts
└── layout.tsx           # (optional) Feature layout

src/features/auth/
├── components/
│   ├── login-form.tsx
│   ├── register-form.tsx
│   └── auth-tab-switch.tsx
```

**When adding new UI:** Create a feature subdirectory. Don't scatter components across multiple folders.

### 2. Pages & Layouts (`app/`)

Uses Next.js 16 App Router with directory-based routing:

- `app/page.tsx` — home
- `app/dashboard/page.tsx` — `/dashboard` route
- `app/(auth)/login/page.tsx` — grouped route, `/login` (auth group doesn't appear in URL)
- `layout.tsx` at any level applies to all child routes

**Pattern:** Page files are thin; they compose feature components and handle top-level layout logic. Business logic lives in features.

### 3. Shared Components (`src/shared/components/`)

**UI Primitives** (`ui/`): Base components from shadcn/Base UI

- `button.tsx`, `input.tsx`, `dialog.tsx`, `select.tsx`, etc.
- These are rarely modified—styled with Tailwind + CVA (class-variance-authority)

**Guards** (`guard/`): Route protection and conditional rendering

- `auth-guard.tsx` — wraps children, redirects if unauthenticated
- Integrates with auth context for user state

**Providers** (`providers/`): Root-level providers

- `providers.tsx` — Apollo Client, Auth context

### 4. GraphQL API Integration

**Pattern:** Query-per-file, auto-generated hooks and types

```
src/shared/api/
├── *.graphql              # Query/mutation definitions
│   ├── login.graphql
│   ├── changeOrderStatus.graphql
│   └── exchangeRateList.graphql
└── *.schemas.tsx          # Auto-generated by ./scripts/codegen.js
    ├── login.schemas.tsx  → exports useLoginMutation, LoginMutationVariables, etc.
```

**How it works:**

1. Define GraphQL in `.graphql` files
2. Run `pnpm codegen` → generates `.schemas.tsx` hooks and types
3. Import in components: `import { useLoginMutation, LoginMutationVariables } from '../../../shared/login.schemas'`

**Do NOT manually edit `.schemas.tsx`** — they're regenerated on codegen.

### 5. Core Utilities

| Module                     | Purpose                               | Key Exports                  |
| -------------------------- | ------------------------------------- | ---------------------------- |
| `src/lib/auth-context.tsx` | Auth state & methods                  | `useAuth()`, `AuthContext`   |
| `src/lib/tokens.ts`        | Local storage access token management | `getAccess()`, `setAccess()` |
| `src/lib/apollo.ts`        | Apollo Client config                  | configured client instance   |

## Key Architecture Decisions

### Client-Side Rendering (CSR)

**Current approach:** Most features use client-side rendering with Apollo hooks (e.g., `useOrderListByStatusQuery`, `usePlaceOrderMutation`).

**Why:** Real-time dashboards, authenticated mutations, form state. Server-side rendering adds complexity for this use case.

**When you build components:**

- Use `'use client'` at feature level if the component uses hooks
- Fetch data in components with Apollo hooks
- Handle loading/error states in the component

### Auth Flow

1. User logs in → GraphQL mutation saves access token in local storage (`src/lib/tokens.ts`)
2. Apollo Client reads token and adds to GraphQL headers automatically (`src/lib/apollo.ts`)
3. Protected routes use `AuthGuard` wrapper
4. `useAuth()` hook provides `user`, `isLoading`, `refreshAuth()`

### Dependency Conventions

- **UI**: Tailwind (`@tailwindcss/postcss@4`), class-variance-authority (CVA)
- **Forms**: Custom React hooks (useState, useCallback)—no form library yet
- **GraphQL**: Apollo Client, auto-code-generated hooks
- **Navigation**: Next.js built-in (useRouter, Link)

## Common Patterns

### Creating a New Feature

```typescript
// 1. Create directory structure
src/features/my-feature/
├── components/my-component.tsx
├── hooks/use-my-hook.ts
└── utils/helpers.ts

// 2. Create component with 'use client' if it has state
// src/features/my-feature/components/my-component.tsx
'use client';

import { useMyQuery } from '../../../shared/api/myquery.schemas';

export function MyComponent() {
  const { data, loading, error } = useMyQuery();
  // render...
}

// 3. Use in page
// app/my-route/page.tsx
import { MyComponent } from '@/features/my-feature/components/my-component';

export default function Page() {
  return <MyComponent />;
}
```

### Adding a GraphQL Mutation

```graphql
# src/shared/api/myMutation.graphql
mutation MyMutation($input: MyInput!) {
  myMutation(input: $input) {
    id
    name
  }
}
```

Then run:

```bash
pnpm codegen
```

This generates `src/shared/myMutation.schemas.tsx` with:

- `useMyMutationMutation()` hook
- `MyMutationMutation` type
- `MyMutationMutationVariables` type

Import and use: `const [myMutation, { loading }] = useMyMutationMutation();`

### Form State Pattern (No Library Yet)

Use local `useState` for form inputs, manual submission:

```typescript
const [quantity, setQuantity] = useState('');
const [mutation, { loading }] = useSomeMutation();

async function handleSubmit(e: React.FormEvent) {
  e.preventDefault();
  try {
    await mutation({ variables: { quantity } });
  } catch (err) {
    const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Error';
    toast.error(msg);
  }
}
```

See: `src/features/dashboard/components/place-order-button.tsx` for full example.

## Build & Deployment

**Local development:**

```bash
pnpm install
pnpm dev              # Start dev server at localhost:3000
pnpm build            # Build for production
pnpm lint             # Run ESLint
pnpm check-types      # Type check all files
pnpm codegen          # Generate GraphQL schemas
```

**Docker deployment:**

```bash
docker build --platform linux/amd64 \
  --build-arg NEXT_PUBLIC_GRAPHQL_URL=<your-graphql-url> \
  -t your-image:tag .
```

See: `Dockerfile`, `next.config.ts`

## Linting & Formatting

| Tool       | Config                  | Purpose                                            |
| ---------- | ----------------------- | -------------------------------------------------- |
| ESLint     | `eslint.config.mjs`     | TS/JSX linting, import sorting, object key sorting |
| Prettier   | `.prettierrc.json`      | Code formatting (integrated with Tailwind plugin)  |
| Husky      | `.husky/`               | Git hooks (lint-staged, type-check on commit)      |
| CommitLint | `commitlint.config.mjs` | Enforce conventional commit messages               |

**Before committing:**

```bash
# Runs automatically via husky
# - ESLint --fix
# - Prettier
# - Type check
```

## TypeScript

- Strict mode enabled in `tsconfig.json`
- Auto-generated GraphQL types are strict
- Import sorting: alphabetical by `eslint-plugin-simple-import-sort`
- Type exports for schemas live in `*.schemas.tsx` (auto-generated)

## Performance Notes

- **Next.js 16+** uses React 19 with automatic batching
- **Apollo Client** caches queries; mutations invalidate cache (see cache policies in mutation calls)
- **Tailwind 4** with CSS variables for tokens (check `globals.css`)
- **Images**: Use `next/image` for optimization (if applicable)

## Important: Next.js Breaking Changes

⚠️ **This project uses Next.js 16.2.2** — APIs differ from older versions.

Before writing code:

1. Check the official [Next.js 16 docs](https://nextjs.org/docs)
2. Focus on App Router patterns (directory-based routing in `app/`)
3. Note changes in data fetching, middleware, and rendering modes

See: `AGENTS.md` for the detailed warning.

## Next Steps

- **Ready to explore the codebase?** Start with `src/features/dashboard/` or `src/features/auth/` for concrete examples
- **Adding a new page?** Create a new directory in `app/` and compose feature components
- **Creating a GraphQL query?** Add `.graphql` file in `src/shared/api/`, run codegen, then use in a feature component
- **Building a shared component?** Add to `src/shared/components/` with TypeScript types and Tailwind styling
